task:
ok, so now that we have that flow correct, what happens when 5 files are uploaded to a folder at the same time by a user in the web app?

- How many IPFS pins
- How many IPNS publishes

Can any of these be batched or simplified - eg. only pinning new folder metadata once for the batch, and only publishing the folder ipns once.
Ok given that we are less constrained by IPFS infra having tested 200 concurrent users, I think the efficiency gains from this justify making these changes - please /gsd:add-phase to deal with this.
user_feedback_text: Ok given that we are less constrained by IPFS infra having tested 200 concurrent users, I think the efficiency gains from this justify making these changes - please /gsd:add-phase to deal with this.

Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@tests/web-e2e/page-objects/file-browser/file-list.page.ts` around lines 23 -
33, The selector string '.file-list-item:not(.upload-inline-row)' is duplicated;
extract it to a class-level constant (e.g. private static readonly ROW_SELECTOR
= '.file-list-item:not(.upload-inline-row)') and replace the literal in
getItem(name: string) and the other method that currently returns
this.page.locator('.file-list-item:not(.upload-inline-row)') to use that
constant (this.constructor.ROW_SELECTOR or ClassName.ROW_SELECTOR depending on
instance/static choice) so both locators share a single source of truth.
user_feedback_text:
